votes.reason: The Python code directly implements the recursive logic and helper functions defined in the Coq code.
The Python code directly translates the Coq definitions and recursive functions, preserving the logic for checking odd values at even indices and summing them.
The Python code correctly implements the recursive logic to sum odd integers at even indices, matching the Coq specification.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. is_odd checks if a number is odd (Z.odd in Coq returns true for odd numbers, which matches n % 2 != 0 in Python). is_even_pos checks if an index is even using Nat.even, which matches i % 2 == 0. The recursive sum_odd_at_even_positions_aux function traverses the list with an index, adding elements that are odd and at even positions, exactly as in Coq. The solution_spec returns a boolean comparison matching the Coq Prop equality.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. is_odd checks if a number is odd (Z.odd in Coq returns true for odd numbers, which matches n % 2 != 0 in Python). is_even_pos checks if an index is even using Nat.even in Coq and i % 2 == 0 in Python. The recursive sum_odd_at_even_positions_aux function traverses the list, incrementing the index, and sums elements that are odd and at even positions, matching the Coq logic exactly. The solution_spec returns a boolean comparison in Python versus a Prop equality in Coq, but for concrete inputs they produce the same judgment.

The recursive sum_odd_at_even_positions_aux function traverses the list, incrementing the index, and sums elements that are odd and at even positions, matching the Coq logic exactly. The solution_spec returns a boolean comparison in Python versus a Prop equality in Coq, but for concrete inputs they produce equivalent judgments.
Both compute the sum of elements at even 0-based positions that are odd; parity checks for integers (including negatives) and even indices match Coq’s Z.odd and Nat.even, and recursion/order align.

Both implementations sum elements at even indices (0-based) when the element is odd; recursion, parity checks (including negatives), and initial index match. solution_spec checks equality to this sum in both.
Both sum elements that are odd at even (0-based) positions, with identical parity checks (including negatives), recursion, and base cases.
